PhillyStat360 — 04b: Model Validation & Spatial Analysis

MUSA Practicum Team

2026-04-19

library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(scales)
library(pROC)
library(sf)
library(tidycensus)
library(patchwork)

.root      <- if (dir.exists("PhillyStat360")) "PhillyStat360" else ".."
data_path  <- paste0(.root, "/rawdata/")
feat_path  <- paste0(.root, "/data/")
graph_path <- paste0(.root, "/graphs/")
dir.create(graph_path, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

1. Load Data

# tar_read() when running via tar_make(); falls back to CSV for standalone knit.
preds <- tryCatch(
  targets::tar_read(predictions_data),
  error = function(e)
    read_csv(paste0(feat_path, "all_predictions_rf.csv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
)

cat("Predictions loaded:", comma(nrow(preds)), "parcels\n")
## Predictions loaded: 520,196 parcels
features_full <- tryCatch(
  targets::tar_read(features_data),
  error = function(e)
    read_csv(paste0(feat_path, "features_residential.csv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
)

# features_residential: has zip_code, census_tract
features <- features_full %>%
  select(parcel_number, zip_code, census_tract,
         exterior_condition, building_age)

# OPA: geographic_ward, building_code_description, category_code_description
opa <- read_csv(paste0(data_path, "opa_properties_public.csv"),
                show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  clean_names() %>%
  select(parcel_number, geographic_ward,
         category_code, category_code_description,
         building_code_description) %>%
  mutate(parcel_number = as.character(parcel_number))

cat("OPA loaded:", comma(nrow(opa)), "parcels\n")
## OPA loaded: 583,802 parcels
tier_levels <- c(
  "Very Unlikely (0–0.2)",
  "Unlikely (0.2–0.4)",
  "Maybe (0.4–0.6)",
  "Likely (0.6–0.8)",
  "Very Likely (0.8–1.0)"
)

df <- preds %>%
  left_join(features, by = "parcel_number") %>%
  left_join(opa,      by = "parcel_number") %>%
  mutate(
    prob_tier = case_when(
      rf_prob >= 0.8 ~ "Very Likely (0.8–1.0)",
      rf_prob >= 0.6 ~ "Likely (0.6–0.8)",
      rf_prob >= 0.4 ~ "Maybe (0.4–0.6)",
      rf_prob >= 0.2 ~ "Unlikely (0.2–0.4)",
      TRUE           ~ "Very Unlikely (0–0.2)"
    ),
    prob_tier = factor(prob_tier, levels = tier_levels)
  )

cat("Assembled:", comma(nrow(df)), "parcels\n")
## Assembled: 520,196 parcels
cat("zip_code coverage:       ", percent(mean(!is.na(df$zip_code)), 0.1), "\n")
## zip_code coverage:        100.0%
cat("geographic_ward coverage:", percent(mean(!is.na(df$geographic_ward)), 0.1), "\n")
## geographic_ward coverage: 100.0%

2. Generalization Check: AUC by Zip Code

zip_auc <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(zip_code)) %>%
  group_by(zip_code) %>%
  filter(n() >= 50, sum(ovs == 1) >= 5) %>%
  summarise(
    n_parcels = n(),
    n_vacant  = sum(ovs == 1),
    prev_rate = mean(ovs == 1),
    auc       = as.numeric(auc(roc(ovs, rf_prob, quiet = TRUE))),
    .groups   = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(auc)

cat("Zip codes with sufficient data:", nrow(zip_auc), "\n")
## Zip codes with sufficient data: 41
cat("Median AUC:", round(median(zip_auc$auc), 3), "\n")
## Median AUC: 0.824
cat("5th percentile AUC:", round(quantile(zip_auc$auc, 0.05), 3), "\n")
## 5th percentile AUC: 0.698
cat("Zips with AUC < 0.70:", sum(zip_auc$auc < 0.70), "\n")
## Zips with AUC < 0.70: 3
zip_auc %>%
  head(10) %>%
  mutate(
    prev_rate = percent(prev_rate, 0.1),
    auc       = round(auc, 3)
  ) %>%
  kable(
    col.names = c("Zip", "N Parcels", "N Vacant", "Vacancy Rate", "AUC"),
    caption   = "10 Lowest-AUC Zip Codes (min 50 parcels, 5 positives)"
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE)
10 Lowest-AUC Zip Codes (min 50 parcels, 5 positives)
Zip N Parcels N Vacant Vacancy Rate AUC
19123 5745 23 0.4% 0.672
19147 15245 61 0.4% 0.687
19148 20472 132 0.6% 0.698
19152 10076 11 0.1% 0.725
19125 11960 93 0.8% 0.729
19122 6581 35 0.5% 0.753
19130 10327 31 0.3% 0.756
19145 17827 137 0.8% 0.760
19115 10126 13 0.1% 0.768
19129 3902 29 0.7% 0.791 
p_zip_auc <- zip_auc %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(zip_code, auc), y = auc, fill = auc)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.70, linetype = "dashed",
             color = "tomato", linewidth = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "tomato", high = "steelblue", name = "AUC") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1),
                     labels = number_format(accuracy = 0.01)) +
  annotate("text", x = 3, y = 0.73,
           label = "AUC = 0.70", color = "tomato", size = 3.5) +
  labs(
    title    = "RF Model AUC by Zip Code",
    subtitle = paste0(nrow(zip_auc), " zip codes | min 50 parcels, 5 positives"),
    x = "Zip Code", y = "AUC"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(axis.text.x  = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, size = 7),
        legend.position = "none")

p_zip_auc

ggsave(paste0(graph_path, "auc_by_zip.png"),
       plot = p_zip_auc, width = 12, height = 6, dpi = 300)

3. Generalization Check: AUC by Building Type

bldg_auc <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(category_code_description)) %>%
  group_by(category_code_description) %>%
  filter(n() >= 100, sum(ovs == 1) >= 10) %>%
  summarise(
    n_parcels = n(),
    n_vacant  = sum(ovs == 1),
    prev_rate = mean(ovs == 1),
    auc       = as.numeric(auc(roc(ovs, rf_prob, quiet = TRUE))),
    .groups   = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(n_parcels))

low_auc_rows <- which(bldg_auc$auc < 0.70)

bldg_auc %>%
  mutate(
    prev_rate = percent(prev_rate, 0.1),
    auc       = round(auc, 3),
    n_parcels = comma(n_parcels),
    n_vacant  = comma(n_vacant)
  ) %>%
  kable(
    col.names = c("Building Type", "N Parcels", "N Vacant",
                  "Vacancy Rate", "AUC"),
    caption   = "RF Model AUC by Building Type"
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  row_spec(low_auc_rows, background = "#fff3e0")
RF Model AUC by Building Type
Building Type N Parcels N Vacant Vacancy Rate AUC
SINGLE FAMILY 461,662 4,956 1.1% 0.846
MULTI FAMILY 40,892 603 1.5% 0.853
MIXED USE 14,021 344 2.5% 0.799
APARTMENTS > 4 UNITS 3,621 31 0.9% 0.768

4. Probability Tier Distribution

Parcels are classified into five tiers following Tim Haynes’ specification. Tier counts and OVS confirmation rates are reported; no binary threshold is applied.

tier_colors <- c(
  "Very Unlikely (0–0.2)"  = "#4575b4",
  "Unlikely (0.2–0.4)"     = "#91bfdb",
  "Maybe (0.4–0.6)"        = "#ffffbf",
  "Likely (0.6–0.8)"       = "#fc8d59",
  "Very Likely (0.8–1.0)"  = "#d73027"
)

tier_summary <- df %>%
  group_by(prob_tier) %>%
  summarise(
    n_parcels = n(),
    n_ovs     = sum(ovs == 1),
    ovs_rate  = mean(ovs == 1),
    mean_prob = mean(rf_prob),
    .groups   = "drop"
  )

tier_summary %>%
  mutate(
    pct      = percent(n_parcels / sum(n_parcels), 0.1),
    n_parcels = comma(n_parcels),
    n_ovs    = comma(n_ovs),
    ovs_rate = percent(ovs_rate, 0.1),
    mean_prob = round(mean_prob, 3)
  ) %>%
  kable(
    col.names = c("Tier", "N Parcels", "N OVS Confirmed",
                  "OVS Rate in Tier", "Mean P(vacant)", "% of Total"),
    caption   = "Probability Tier Distribution — All Residential Parcels"
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE)
Probability Tier Distribution — All Residential Parcels
Tier N Parcels N OVS Confirmed OVS Rate in Tier Mean P(vacant) % of Total
Very Unlikely (0–0.2) 516,802 3,330 0.6% 0.001 99.3%
Unlikely (0.2–0.4) 1,425 991 69.5% 0.306 0.3%
Maybe (0.4–0.6) 823 614 74.6% 0.482 0.2%
Likely (0.6–0.8) 460 367 79.8% 0.710 0.1%
Very Likely (0.8–1.0) 686 632 92.1% 0.878 0.1% 
p_tier_count <- tier_summary %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = prob_tier, y = n_parcels, fill = prob_tier)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = comma(n_parcels)),
            vjust = -0.4, size = 3.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = tier_colors) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma, expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.12))) +
  labs(
    title = "Parcel Count by Probability Tier",
    x = NULL, y = "N Parcels"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9))

p_tier_ovs <- tier_summary %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = prob_tier, y = ovs_rate, fill = prob_tier)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = percent(ovs_rate, 0.1)),
            vjust = -0.4, size = 3.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = tier_colors) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent, expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))) +
  labs(
    title = "OVS Confirmation Rate by Tier",
    subtitle = "Higher tiers show higher observed vacancy rates",
    x = NULL, y = "OVS Rate"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9))

p_tier_count / p_tier_ovs

ggsave(paste0(graph_path, "tier_distribution.png"),
       plot = p_tier_count / p_tier_ovs,
       width = 10, height = 9, dpi = 300)

5. Ward-Level Summary

ward_summary <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(geographic_ward)) %>%
  group_by(geographic_ward) %>%
  summarise(
    n_parcels    = n(),
    n_ovs        = sum(ovs == 1),
    ovs_rate     = mean(ovs == 1),
    mean_rf_prob = mean(rf_prob),
    n_very_likely = sum(prob_tier == "Very Likely (0.8–1.0)"),
    n_likely      = sum(prob_tier == "Likely (0.6–0.8)"),
    n_high_risk   = sum(prob_tier %in% c("Likely (0.6–0.8)",
                                          "Very Likely (0.8–1.0)")),
    high_risk_rate = mean(prob_tier %in% c("Likely (0.6–0.8)",
                                            "Very Likely (0.8–1.0)")),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_rf_prob))

ward_summary %>%
  head(15) %>%
  mutate(
    n_parcels      = comma(n_parcels),
    n_ovs          = comma(n_ovs),
    ovs_rate       = percent(ovs_rate, 0.1),
    mean_rf_prob   = round(mean_rf_prob, 4),
    n_very_likely  = comma(n_very_likely),
    n_likely       = comma(n_likely),
    high_risk_rate = percent(high_risk_rate, 0.1)
  ) %>%
  kable(
    col.names = c("Ward", "N Parcels", "OVS Flagged", "OVS Rate",
                  "Mean P(vacant)", "Very Likely", "Likely",
                  "High Risk (≥0.6)", "High Risk Rate"),
    caption   = "Top 15 Wards by Mean RF Probability"
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  row_spec(1, bold = TRUE, background = "#fde8e8")
Top 15 Wards by Mean RF Probability
Ward N Parcels OVS Flagged OVS Rate Mean P(vacant) Very Likely Likely High Risk (≥0.6) High Risk Rate
11 4,711 217 4.6% 0.0193 41 19 60 1.3%
16 4,325 158 3.7% 0.0189 40 19 59 1.4%
28 4,922 189 3.8% 0.0154 36 15 51 1.0%
44 5,338 191 3.6% 0.0134 26 18 44 0.8%
13 6,977 204 2.9% 0.0128 36 17 53 0.8%
6 4,094 138 3.4% 0.0125 19 9 28 0.7%
32 6,343 222 3.5% 0.0116 26 16 42 0.7%
37 4,600 143 3.1% 0.0115 21 15 36 0.8%
51 8,003 208 2.6% 0.0092 23 15 38 0.5%
4 7,360 200 2.7% 0.0091 23 11 34 0.5%
12 6,454 151 2.3% 0.0091 19 14 33 0.5%
38 6,433 135 2.1% 0.0082 26 8 34 0.5%
43 7,226 139 1.9% 0.0082 22 16 38 0.5%
24 3,717 86 2.3% 0.0081 10 7 17 0.5%
3 6,953 155 2.2% 0.0080 19 11 30 0.4% 
p_ward <- ward_summary %>%
  slice_max(mean_rf_prob, n = 20) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(geographic_ward, mean_rf_prob),
             y = mean_rf_prob)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#d73027", width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(mean_rf_prob, 3)),
            hjust = -0.1, size = 3) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))) +
  labs(
    title    = "Top 20 Wards by Mean RF Vacancy Probability",
    x = "Ward", y = "Mean P(vacant)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

p_ward

ggsave(paste0(graph_path, "ward_mean_prob.png"),
       plot = p_ward, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

6. Spatial Maps

parcel_sf <- st_read(paste0(data_path, "PWD_PARCELS.geojson"),
                     quiet = TRUE) %>%
  clean_names() %>%
  select(brt_id, geometry) %>%
  mutate(brt_id = as.character(brt_id))

cat("Parcel geometry:", comma(nrow(parcel_sf)), "parcels\n")
## Parcel geometry: 547,220 parcels
pred_sf <- parcel_sf %>%
  inner_join(
    df %>% select(parcel_number, rf_prob, prob_tier, ovs, zip_code),
    by = c("brt_id" = "parcel_number")
  )

cat("Matched:", comma(nrow(pred_sf)), "parcels with geometry\n")
## Matched: 485,906 parcels with geometry
p_map_prob <- pred_sf %>%
  arrange(rf_prob) %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = rf_prob), color = NA, linewidth = 0) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#f7f7f7", high = "#d73027",
                      name = "P(vacant)",
                      labels = number_format(accuracy = 0.01)) +
  labs(
    title    = "RF Predicted Vacancy Probability",
    subtitle = "All residential parcels"
  ) +
  theme_void(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

p_map_prob

ggsave(paste0(graph_path, "map_rf_probability.png"),
       plot = p_map_prob, width = 10, height = 9, dpi = 300)
p_map_tiers <- pred_sf %>%
  arrange(prob_tier) %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = prob_tier), color = NA, linewidth = 0) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = tier_colors, name = "Tier",
                    drop = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title    = "RF Vacancy Probability Tiers",
    subtitle = "Five tiers per Tim Haynes classification"
  ) +
  theme_void(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

p_map_tiers

ggsave(paste0(graph_path, "map_rf_tiers.png"),
       plot = p_map_tiers, width = 10, height = 9, dpi = 300)
p_maps <- p_map_prob | p_map_tiers
p_maps

ggsave(paste0(graph_path, "maps_combined.png"),
       plot = p_maps, width = 18, height = 9, dpi = 300)

7. Equity Audit: Performance by Neighborhood Poverty

logic_model.Rmd documents that L&I inspection rates correlate with neighborhood demographics (Kontokosta, Hong & Korsberg 2017). This section quantifies whether model performance differs systematically by census-tract poverty level — a proxy for the inspection-rate bias the model may have inherited from its training labels.

# Fetch ACS 5-year poverty rate at census-tract level (same join as 04a)
acs_poverty <- tryCatch({
  get_acs(
    geography = "tract",
    variables = c(poverty_rate = "S1701_C03_001"),
    state     = "PA",
    county    = "Philadelphia",
    year      = 2022,
    survey    = "acs5"
  ) %>%
    clean_names() %>%
    select(geoid, poverty_rate = estimate) %>%
    mutate(census_tract_key = str_sub(geoid, -6, -1))
}, error = function(e) {
  message("ACS fetch failed: ", e$message)
  NULL
})
if (!is.null(acs_poverty)) {
  # Align OPA census_tract integer format (e.g. 153) to ACS 6-digit key (015300)
  df_eq <- df %>%
    mutate(census_tract_key = str_pad(
      as.character(as.integer(census_tract) * 100), 6, pad = "0"
    )) %>%
    left_join(acs_poverty %>% select(census_tract_key, poverty_rate),
              by = "census_tract_key")

  cat("Parcels with poverty_rate:", comma(sum(!is.na(df_eq$poverty_rate))),
      "/", comma(nrow(df_eq)), "\n")
} else {
  df_eq <- df %>% mutate(poverty_rate = NA_real_)
  cat("ACS fetch failed — equity audit skipped\n")
}
## Parcels with poverty_rate: 336,563 / 520,196
if (sum(!is.na(df_eq$poverty_rate)) > nrow(df_eq) * 0.5) {

  df_eq <- df_eq %>%
    mutate(poverty_quintile = ntile(poverty_rate, 5),
           poverty_quintile = factor(poverty_quintile,
                                     labels = c("Q1 (lowest)", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4",
                                                "Q5 (highest)")))

  equity_summary <- df_eq %>%
    group_by(poverty_quintile) %>%
    summarise(
      n_parcels     = n(),
      n_vacant      = sum(ovs == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
      obs_rate      = mean(ovs == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
      mean_pred     = mean(rf_prob, na.rm = TRUE),
      auc           = tryCatch(
        as.numeric(pROC::auc(pROC::roc(ovs, rf_prob, quiet = TRUE))),
        error = function(e) NA_real_
      ),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(across(c(obs_rate, mean_pred), ~ round(.x, 4)),
           auc = round(auc, 3))

  equity_summary %>%
    kable(col.names = c("Poverty Quintile", "Parcels", "Observed Vacant",
                        "Observed Rate", "Mean P(vacant)", "AUC"),
          caption = "Model performance by census-tract poverty quintile") %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed")) %>%
    row_spec(which(equity_summary$auc < 0.70), background = "#ffd6d6")

} else {
  cat("Insufficient poverty_rate coverage — equity table skipped\n")
}
Model performance by census-tract poverty quintile
Poverty Quintile Parcels Observed Vacant Observed Rate Mean P(vacant) AUC
Q1 (lowest) 67313 277 0.0041 0.0010 0.773
Q2 67313 467 0.0069 0.0023 0.824
Q3 67313 765 0.0114 0.0041 0.841
Q4 67312 1056 0.0157 0.0059 0.853
Q5 (highest) 67312 1559 0.0232 0.0088 0.860
NA 183633 1810 0.0099 0.0032 0.839 
if (exists("equity_summary") && !all(is.na(equity_summary$auc))) {

  p_eq <- equity_summary %>%
    pivot_longer(c(obs_rate, mean_pred), names_to = "metric", values_to = "value") %>%
    mutate(metric = recode(metric,
                           obs_rate  = "Observed vacancy rate",
                           mean_pred = "Mean predicted P(vacant)")) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = poverty_quintile, y = value, fill = metric)) +
    geom_col(position = "dodge") +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(accuracy = 0.1)) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("#2166ac", "#d6604d")) +
    labs(title = "Observed vs. predicted vacancy rate by neighborhood poverty",
         subtitle = "Systematic divergence signals inspection-rate bias inherited from training labels",
         x = "Census-tract poverty quintile (ACS 2022)",
         y = NULL, fill = NULL) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

  p_eq
  ggsave(paste0(graph_path, "equity_poverty_quintile.png"),
         plot = p_eq, width = 8, height = 4, dpi = 300)

}

Interpretation: If AUC or recall drops materially in low-poverty quintiles (Q1–Q2), it indicates the model is less able to identify vacancy in neighborhoods with historically fewer L&I inspections — not because vacancy is rarer there, but because the administrative labels are sparser. Flag this as a known limitation in stakeholder-facing outputs.

8. Export

df %>%
  select(parcel_number, ovs, data_split,
         ovs_clean_seal, ovs_violation, ovs_license,
         rf_prob, logit_prob, prob_tier,
         zip_code, geographic_ward, census_tract,
         category_code, category_code_description) %>%
  write_csv(paste0(feat_path, "predictions_04b.csv"))

cat("Exported: predictions_04b.csv —", comma(nrow(df)), "rows\n")
## Exported: predictions_04b.csv — 520,196 rows
ward_summary %>%
  write_csv(paste0(feat_path, "ward_summary_04b.csv"))

cat("Exported: ward_summary_04b.csv\n")
## Exported: ward_summary_04b.csv
zip_auc %>%
  write_csv(paste0(feat_path, "zip_auc_04b.csv"))

cat("Exported: zip_auc_04b.csv\n")
## Exported: zip_auc_04b.csv
if (exists("equity_summary")) {
  equity_summary %>%
    write_csv(paste0(feat_path, "equity_poverty_auc.csv"))
  cat("Exported: equity_poverty_auc.csv\n")
}
## Exported: equity_poverty_auc.csv
